title: Add support for removing image labels present from parent images
body:
**Description**

Images build from other parent images will inherit the labels in those images. If the protocols for building both are not the same, there is a risk that image labels from the first will bleed through to the second image. 

One potential solution (mirroring the functionality of `--unsetenv`) has been created for this before I could make the issue -- https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/5062, but I wanted to create the issue tracker as well since the request came from me.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Build a container from a parent image with labels, adding none
2. See the image labels present on the resulting container


**Describe the results you received:**


**Describe the results you expected:**

comments.body: From my perspective, I prefer https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/5062 to the original suggested implementation https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/5061 as it requires explicit actions to remove labels. This will reduce the likelihood that labels are unintentionally removed that were present on the parent image.
